Special sets of Insulated Rail Joints <IRJ> exist at each end of the incline single line. No train or vehicle should stand over these IRJ simultaneously; however, it is possible for legitimate non-conflicting routes to be established from Kings Cross <KX> and Camden Road Signalboxes which would enable this situation.~ To avoid this, the KX signaller must immediately be advised if a train is positioned, or to be moved, over the IRJ between Track Circuits <TC> T1232 & T1213 at Camden Road or if the IRJ between these TC fails. The KX signaller must not then allow a movement across the IRJ between TC XNB & T.007 on KX panel.~ Equally, if KX advises that a train is positioned or to be moved over the IRJ between <TC> XNB & T.007 or that the IRJ has failed, no movement is to be authorised across the IRJ between TC T1232 & T1213 at Camden Road~ Special arrangements will need to be considered by the Possession Planner/PICOP if one or more of these IRJ?s are included within a Possession.

Confirmed Bat roost within the Signal Box. Bat survey undertaken by JBA in 2013 prior to the decommissioning of the signal box identified pippistrelle bat droppings within the roof void of the signal box. The droppings were found within the main roof void and the void above the kitchen. Three bat emergence surveys were undertaken but no bats were recorded emerging. It was concluded that the building has been in use as a summer bat roost. A bat licence from Natural England would be required if this building were to be demolished or works were undertaken that would affect the roof or the roof void. The bat reports can be accessed via Moss, type into the search National Records Group Ecology Studies and then look for a report titled Ely-Norwich Re-signalling - Bat Activity Survey Shippea Hill.
